---
title: "World's Fairs"
---

```{r,warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, error=FALSE}
  library(tidyverse)
  tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2024-08-13')
  worlds_fairs <- tuesdata$worlds_fairs
  ggplot(data = worlds_fairs, 
         aes(x = start_year, y = visitors, color = attending_countries)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "World's Fairs: Year vs Visitors",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Number of Visitors",
       color = "Attending Countries") +
  theme_minimal()

```

World's Fairs visitor numbers are up since 1850! Also, the color gradient shows the increase in the number of countries who attend the World's Fair.

World's Fairs Data came from Tidy Tuesday: <https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2024/2024-08-13/readme.md>

-data was collected from the wikipedia page to analyze trends related to the fair.
